skill.name: sales-enablement
skill.description: Create practical B2B sales enablement assets, including pitch decks, one-pagers, objection handling guides, demo scripts, ROI calculators, proposals, playbooks, and persona cards.
skill.summary: Build structured sales collateral and talk tracks for B2B sales teams.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from sales terminology, Markdown examples, and ordinary prose. No evidence found of executable code, command execution, network activity, secret access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords in Sales Prose
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings match ordinary sales enablement language such as proof, pricing, slide structure, ROI, and objection handling. These files are Markdown or eval content and do not implement cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are natural-language sales instructions and examples, not code. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or key handling is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are triple-backtick Markdown fences used to show one-pager layout templates. They are not Ruby shell backtick execution and contain no command strings or runtime path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The one-pager locations are Markdown code blocks containing document layout placeholders. SKILL.md line 15 only instructs reading local product marketing context files and does not execute a shell command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms Used in Business Context
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are ordinary sales discovery and technical-evaluation wording. They ask for buyer context or describe architecture discussion topics, not system or network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are sales or presentation guidance and contain no instructions to enumerate hosts, ports, services, users, or local system details.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Script Pattern in Natural Language
low_findings.description: The scripts finding points to a case study structure line in Markdown. It uses ordinary prose for sales case study formatting and does not contain a deprecated programming with statement.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context is a numbered Markdown list item describing challenge text length. It is not JavaScript, Python, or another executable script syntax.

content.value_statement: Sales teams need clear materials that support each deal stage and buyer role. This skill turns product context into decks, one-pagers, objection docs, demo scripts, ROI tools, playbooks, and proposals.
actual_capabilities: Creates slide-by-slide sales deck outlines with speaker notes and proof points.
Drafts one-pagers and leave-behinds for product, use-case, meeting recap, and champion enablement needs.
Builds objection handling tables with response approaches, proof points, and follow-up questions.
Structures demo scripts with timing, talk tracks, interaction points, and next steps.
Designs ROI calculator inputs, formulas, outputs, and value messaging by persona.
Outlines proposals, sales playbooks, case study briefs, and buyer persona cards.
limitations: Requires accurate product, buyer, pricing, and proof-point context from the user.
Does not verify customer claims, legal claims, security certifications, or financial projections.
Does not create finished visual design files or branded slide decks by itself.
Public competitor comparison pages are outside its scope and should use a comparison-focused skill.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New Product Launch Kit
use_cases.description: Create a deck, one-pager, demo flow, and objection guide for a launch sales motion.

use_cases.title: Improve Rep Call Readiness
use_cases.description: Build quick-reference talk tracks, objection responses, and proof points for active opportunities.

use_cases.title: Support Enterprise Deal Progression
use_cases.description: Create champion materials, ROI framing, persona-specific messaging, and proposal sections for complex deals.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic One-Pager
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple sales leave-behind.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a one-page sales leave-behind for [product]. Target [persona] at [company type]. Include the problem, solution, three differentiators, one proof point, and a clear next step.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Objection Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an objection handling guide for these objections: [list objections]. For each one, include why the buyer says it, a response approach, a talk track, proof point, and follow-up question.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Persona-Specific Demo
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a timed demo script.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a [duration]-minute demo script for [product] selling to [persona]. Map each scene to their pain points, include interaction questions, proof moments, and a concrete close.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Deal Enablement Package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple coordinated assets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a sales enablement package for [deal or segment]. Include a deck outline, champion one-pager, ROI calculator structure, objection guide, and proposal executive summary. Use this context: [context].

output_examples.input: Create a sales deck for an HR platform selling to HR directors at mid-market companies.
output: A slide-by-slide outline from problem to next steps.
Persona-specific emphasis on engagement, retention, and reporting efficiency.
Speaker notes and proof-point suggestions for each major slide.

output_examples.input: Build an objection guide for budget, timing, and competitor objections.
output: A quick-reference table for live calls.
Detailed talk tracks that acknowledge the concern and redirect to value.
Follow-up questions that keep the deal moving.

output_examples.input: Create a 30-minute demo script for a VP of Marketing.
output: A timed agenda with discovery recap, workflow scenes, proof moments, and close.
Questions to ask during the demo.
Guidance for adapting the flow based on prospect answers.

best_practices: Provide buyer persona, deal stage, sales motion, proof points, and current objections before asking for assets.
Ask for one asset type or one deal package at a time so outputs stay specific.
Validate ROI numbers, customer quotes, and compliance claims before sharing materials externally.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic collateral without naming the buyer, stage, and business problem.
Do not use unverified customer metrics or invented case studies in sales materials.
Do not use this skill for public SEO competitor pages or cold outbound email sequences.

faq.question: What sales assets can this skill create?
faq.answer: It can draft decks, one-pagers, objection guides, demo scripts, ROI calculator structures, proposals, playbooks, and persona cards.

faq.question: Can it create a finished PowerPoint file?
faq.answer: It provides slide structure, copy, speaker notes, and visual guidance. A design or presentation tool is still needed for final files.

faq.question: What context should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide product positioning, target persona, deal stage, sales motion, proof points, common objections, and any existing materials.

faq.question: Can it help with ROI calculators?
faq.answer: Yes. It can define inputs, formulas, assumptions, output views, and persona-specific value messaging.

faq.question: Is this for internal or public sales content?
faq.answer: It is mainly for sales enablement materials used by reps, champions, and prospects during active sales motions.

faq.question: Does it replace legal, finance, or security review?
faq.answer: No. Claims about pricing, ROI, compliance, security, and customer results need review by the responsible internal teams.
